matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Leviticus 26:9:2
matches.x: AND WILL ESTABLISH MY COVENANT WITH YOU. In that you will be as the stars of heaven for multitude (Deut.1:11) and as the dust of the earth (Gen. 13:16).

AND I WILL ESTABLISH MY COVENANT. Rabbi Abraham ibn Ezra said that this is a sign that G-d had sworn to Noah that during the flood, neither he nor his children would die even though it is not clearly written at first. This is similar to what we find in Deuteronomy: And ye came near unto me every one of you, and said: Let us send men before us, that they may search the land for us.49Deuteronomy…

SEND ‘LECHA’ (THEE)1Literally: “for thyself,” as explained by Rashi. MEN. “As you see fit. I do not command you [to send them], but if you wish [to do so], send them. [G-d said this] because the Israelites came [to Moses] and said, Let us send men before us,2Deuteronomy 1:22. The verse continues that the people said to Moses: ‘Let us send men before us, that they may search the Land for us, and…

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Numbers 13:23:1
matches.x: UNTO THE VALLEY OF ESHCOL. These are the words of Moses.35The valley of Eshcol was not yet so called when the spies came there. However, Moses called it by the name which the Israelites later used in referring to it. It is possible that it is similar to and pursued as far as Dan (Gen. 14:14) in that it had another name.36Dan had two names, Dan and Layish (Jud. 18:29). However, Moses in writing…
